skill.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
skill.description: Coordinate multiple AI agents on independent coding tasks without shared state or sequential dependencies.
skill.summary: Dispatch focused parallel agents for independent coding investigations.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences, prose, and task examples as command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance. Manual review found no executable script, no network access, no filesystem access, and no prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Backticks
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown fence delimiters or documentation examples. They are not Ruby backtick expressions and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern matches Markdown code fences in a documentation file. No shell, Ruby, or interpolated command execution syntax is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Flag
low_findings.description: The flagged line is YAML description text for when to use the skill. It contains no cryptographic algorithm, API call, or security implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata prose and not executable code. I found no evidence of weak cryptography usage in the reviewed skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Workflow Prose
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings map to normal workflow instructions about goals and conflict checks. They do not run system or network discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain text bullets inside a process guide. No commands, host discovery, network probing, or data collection behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Sequential debugging slows teams when independent failures can be investigated at the same time. This skill shows how to split work into focused agent tasks, dispatch them in parallel, and integrate results.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when failures are independent enough for parallel agent work.
Defines focused scopes, goals, constraints, and expected outputs for each agent.
Provides example prompts for assigning separate test failures to agents.
Guides review of returned summaries before integrating changes.
Recommends full-suite verification after independent fixes are merged.
limitations: Does not provide executable code or an automation script.
Requires the user to decide whether tasks share state or dependencies.
Works best for coding and test failures, not broad research planning.
Cannot prevent conflicts if agents edit overlapping files.

use_cases.title: Split Independent Test Failures
use_cases.description: Assign unrelated failing test files to separate agents and review each root cause independently.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Multi-Agent Debugging
use_cases.description: Create clear scopes, constraints, and expected outputs before launching several coding agents.

use_cases.title: Reduce Review Bottlenecks
use_cases.description: Collect focused summaries from agents, compare changes, and run final verification before merging.

prompt_templates.title: Find Parallel Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these failures and tell me which ones can be assigned to separate agents. Explain any shared dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Create Agent Tasks
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these independent failures into separate agent prompts. Include scope, goal, constraints, and expected output for each task.

prompt_templates.title: Dispatch With Constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare parallel agent assignments for these test files. Prevent overlap by naming files each agent may inspect or edit.

prompt_templates.title: Integrate Agent Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these agent summaries, identify conflicts, choose an integration order, and define the final verification plan.

output_examples.input: Three unrelated test files fail after a refactor.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends one focused agent per test file, each with a clear scope, constraints, and summary requirement.

output_examples.input: Two failures may share the same root cause.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends investigating them together first, because parallel agents could duplicate work or create conflicting fixes.
The skill recommends checking for file conflicts, reviewing each explanation, and running the full test suite.

best_practices: Use parallel agents only after confirming tasks are independent.
Give each agent one narrow scope and a clear expected summary.
Review every returned change before running final verification.
anti_patterns: Do not assign broad requests such as fixing all tests.
Do not dispatch agents when failures share state or root causes.
Do not integrate agent changes without checking overlap.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps split independent coding failures into focused parallel agent tasks.

faq.question: Does it run agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and prompt structure for tools that support agent dispatch.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples are written for agent workflows in Claude Code and similar coding tools.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. Use it to design independent coding tasks before assigning work to Codex agents.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it when failures are related, require full-system context, or involve overlapping files.

faq.question: How should I verify results?
faq.answer: Read each agent summary, check for conflicts, and run the full test suite.
